name: Scalability Framework

architecture_decisions.decision: Scale expensive and failure-prone workflows independently through queues and workers.
architecture_decisions.rationale: Receipt OCR, translation, media handling, notifications, and provider sync are cost-sensitive and external-service dependent.

architecture_decisions.decision: Optimize first for correctness, scope separation, and auditability before high-scale collaboration.
architecture_decisions.rationale: MVP risks are trust, privacy, and ledger clarity rather than raw traffic volume.

scaling_controls: retry with backoff for external services
manual reconciliation fallback for provider sync
cost_controls: only translate receipts on demand or default trip language need
store display FX snapshots instead of frequent recalculation
media size limits and lifecycle policies
resilience_strategy: core trip workspace remains available when OCR/translation/FX providers fail
external contribution provider outages are surfaced as unavailable or pending external checkout
ledger writes are transactional and audited

risks_and_constraints.risk: AI/document-processing costs may grow faster than monetization.
risks_and_constraints.mitigation: Add usage limits, on-demand processing, and cost telemetry before launch.

stage_4_handoff_notes: Design should account for delayed processing states and retry/manual correction paths.
stage_5_handoff_notes: Implementation planning should include telemetry and limits for OCR, translation, media, notifications, and provider sync.
